records.ety: [Gr. <?/ a thing procured, a deduction from a demonstration, fr. <?/ to bring, provide: cf. F. porisme.]

defs: A proposition affirming the possibility of finding such conditions as will render a certain determinate problem indeterminate or capable of innumerable solutions.

notes: ☞ Three books of porisms of Euclid have been lost, but several attempts to determine the nature of these propositions and to restore them have been made by modern geometers.
